title: leucanthemopsis pallida subsp. alpina
intro: Leucanthemopsis pallida subsp. alpina, also known as the Alpine Daisy or Mountain Daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and skin irritations. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immune-boosting properties. Leucanthemopsis pallida subsp. alpina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote overall well-being.
